message: Retry-decision artifact append failures are silently ignored, so a read-only retry can occur without the required durable JSONL record.
evidence: appendRetryDecisionBestEffort marshals the retry decision and then discards the error from activeStore.AppendBytes; Store.AppendBytes can fail on mkdir/open/write, leaving the required retry-decisions.jsonl record absent while the lifecycle continues.

message: The classifier gives any 5xx token precedence over auth/client errors in ACP internal-error text, so permanent outer auth/client failures that mention an incidental 5xx are retried.
evidence: classifyACPInternalError returns retryable server_error on sig.has5xx before checking sig.hasAuth/sig.hasClientErr; classifyByText repeats the same precedence. The contract requires genuine permanent outer causes to remain non-retryable and 5xx precedence only for genuine outermost 5xx.

message: Retry backoff is exponential but not jittered.
evidence: transportRetryDelay deterministically returns 1s then 2s, and the comment says tests verify exact delay_ms values. The accepted contract requires bounded jittered exponential backoff.

message: The retry-specific tests do not verify that CompletedDespiteTimeout suppresses retries on a read-only stage.
evidence: The new retry branch suppresses retries for CompletedDespiteTimeout, and the contract requires this behavior. The new retry tests cover WallClockTimeout only; existing completed-despite-timeout tests assert success output but not transport call count or absence of retry-decisions, so an accidental retry loop would still pass.

message: The lifecycle tests for context-cancellation retry behavior do not use context.Canceled, so they do not cover the required real error shape.
evidence: TestReadOnlyIdleTimeoutRetryable claims to cover RunResult.TimedOut=true with context.Canceled, but passes errors.New("agent process killed after idle timeout"). The companion no-retry test similarly passes errors.New("context canceled"), so the lifecycle path is not tested with errors.Is-compatible context.Canceled as required by the contract.

message: Retry-decision artifact write failures are silently ignored.
evidence: appendRetryDecisionBestEffort documents that write failures are swallowed and then discards activeStore.AppendBytes errors. The contract requires durable retry-decision JSONL records, so this hides a failed required artifact write.

message: Document the new read-only transport retry behavior and retry-decision artifact; the agent docs still describe reviewer rounds as costing exactly five subprocess runs per reviewer.
evidence: docs/agents.md:527-528 still says five attempts cost five subprocess runs and five usage records, but internal/app/agent_attempt.go:163-220 now loops up to three transport Run calls for ReadOnly stages and writes retry-decisions.jsonl; review, clarify, and draft call sites set ReadOnly=true.
